title: InterDigital awarded another injunction against Disney by Pan-European Court
description: WILMINGTON, Del., July 23, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- InterDigital, Inc. (Nasdaq: IDCC), a wireless, video and AI technology research and development company, today announced that it has been awarded another injunction against Disney by the Unified Patent Court (UPC). The UPC is a pan-European patent court which issues decisions that apply across multiple countries in the European Union (EU).

title: Who is Mykhailo Drapatyi? ‘The Flying BMP’ who now leads Ukraine’s military
description: Zelenskyy names reform-minded Drapatyi as new commander-in-chief, replacing Syrsyki to bend to the largest protests of the war over the firing of Fedorov.

title: Boeing’s MQ-28 Ghost Bat makes Farnborough Airshow debut
description: Boeing's MQ-28 Ghost Bat recently became the first collaborative combat aircraft to participate in a multinational, joint operational exercise.

title: UK’s BAE enters uncrewed fighter competition with ‘Brontanax’
description: BAE Systems revealed a new unmanned fighter jet on Wednesday, boosting Britain’s entry into one of the defense industry’s hottest markets.

title: Unfazed by Iran’s threats, Bulgaria moves to aid U.S. military operations
description: A Bulgarian parliamentary committee has approved a U.S. request to let American forces use a local air base to support the U.S. war against Iran.

title: Backed by France, Macron sees Germany as nuclear-deterrence ‘vanguard’ in Europe
description: A French nuclear-capable Rafale jet had been temporarily hosted at a German air base − specifically at Nörvenich near Cologne.

title: Cost of Iran war rises to $37.5 billion, Pentagon requests $67.1 billion more
description: The Iran war has cost the DoD $37.5 billion to date, Hegseth told senators Tuesday, as he requested $67.1 billion more in emergency funding.

title: New British prime minister gains early defense win with Canada fighter jet tie-up
description: Canada has joined the GCAP fighter program as an observer, marking the first expansion beyond the founding members Britain, Italy and Japan.

title: Iran warns Bulgaria against aiding US military operations
description: Iran’s foreign ministry spokesman warned Bulgaria on Tuesday against allowing the U.S. to use its territory for military operations against Iran.

title: Boeing on track for 2028 Air Force One delivery, costs rise
description: Boeing remains on track to deliver two new Air Force One jets in 2028, but meeting that target will require additional spending.

title: Hanwha Philly Shipyard awarded $2 billion to build 2 missile-tracking vessels
description: The Hanwha Philly Shipyard was awarded $2 billion to build the missile-tracking vessels set to replace the aging Pacific Tracker and Pacific Collector.

title: Germany to study French cooperation offer on SAMP/T air-defense system
description: German government leaders will study an offer by French President Emmanuel Macron to cooperate on the French-Italian SAMP/T air defense system.

title: How startups are reshaping the Pentagon’s drone industry
description: Experts say the Drone Dominance competition reflects a broader shift in how the Pentagon acquires military technology and cultivates defense startups.

title: Pentagon identifies soldier killed in controlled detonation of Iranian drone
description: Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton, 30, was killed Saturday during the controlled detonation of unexploded ordnance from a downed Iranian one-way attack drone.

title: Russia may use captured Ukrainian drones against Poland and Baltics, Warsaw warns
description: Russia has captured a large number of Ukrainian drones and may be planning to use them against NATO or to stage a false flag attack, Polish officials said.

title: US Marine Corps turns to AI-powered Bullfrog as drone threats expand
description: Allen Control Systems announced Monday that the Marine Corps has chosen the Bullfrog autonomous weapon system to integrate into its airborne defenses.

title: X-Bow out to tackle cost quandary with sub-$100,000 drone interceptor
description: X-Bow Systems is launching what it hopes will be a remedy to the ongoing conundrum of combating cheap drones with multimillion dollar interceptors.

title: From depot to detonation: The case for a unified logistics picture
description: "We have the technology for that comprehensive, near real-time common operating picture (COP), we just need to link it," writes LMI's Robert Yon.

title: New executive order tightens defense supply chain waiver rules
description: A new executive order aims to make it harder for U.S. defense contractors to buy critical minerals and other materials from prohibited foreign suppliers.

title: US Air Force begins piloted flights, autonomy tests for modified F-16s
description: Piloted flights and autonomy tests began recently for modified F-16s that allows artificial intelligence agents to fly the aircraft.

title: Doodle Labs eyes US, European production sites as demand soars for jam-resistant drones
description: Doodle Labs hopes to turn its success in equipping jam-hardened drones in Ukraine into additional production sites in Europe and he United States.

title: Pentagon identifies American soldiers killed in Iranian attack in Jordan
description: 1st Lt. Tyler James Feehan, 25, and Pvt. Isabella Gonzales, 19, were killed in action in an Iranian ballistic missile and drone attack in Jordan.

title: New Air Force One to get ‘maxed out’ upgrades after security questions
description: The Qatari-donated Air Force One will be sent away for "maxed out" upgrades, Trump said Sunday in response to questions about security gaps.

title: Lockheed Martin’s Morfius X-Rotor built to fry 50 enemy drones in one flight
description: Lockheed Martin is rolling out the Morfius X-Rotor, a recoverable counter-drone system the company says can kill 50 enemy drones in one flight.

title: Lockheed Martin announces cheaper Patriot interceptor missile
description: Lockheed Martin introduced a cheaper Patriot interceptor as the U.S. and partner nations grapple with the exorbitant costs of shooting down cheap drones.

description: United Launch Alliance sent another batch of Amazon Leo satellites into orbit today. Except for six rockets under contract to Boeing for Starliner missions, it was the last launch of […]

title: Vertex was sole bidder in high-premium Crinetics acquisition
description: Analysts expect new details on the lead-up to the deal “are likely to reignite” investor scrutiny that Vertex overpaid in what’s poised to be its largest-ever transaction.

title: GSK lung cancer drug gets speedy approval following $10.6B deal
description: The clearance is GSK’s first for a lung cancer medicine and the latest step forward in a yearslong push by the British drugmaker into oncology.

title: Arrowhead hits ‘best-case scenario’ with latest results for fat-lowering drug
description: To analysts, the drug’s apparent safety and effects on both triglyceride levels and pancreatitis cases should make it competitive with a rival Ionis therapy.

title: Trump threatens tariffs on imported generic drugs
description: While the possibility of levies on branded pharmaceuticals have had a limited impact on drugmaker revenues, tariffs of up to 200% on copycat medicines could — depending on the specifics — prove more disruptive.

title: Summit counters PD-1/VEGF skeptics; Celldex drug fails skin disease study
description: A new analysis could address some doubts investors have had about ivonescimab, one analyst said. Elsewhere, Gilead and Merck debuted data for a once-weekly HIV tablet and a new pyschedelics competitor emerged.

title: Crystalys nets $130M more to push gout drug through late-stage tests
description: The startup has a broad global development program underway — and the support of more than 20 investment firms — for a treatment that’s approved in many Asian countries but isn’t yet available in the U.S. or Europe.

title: Agios scraps sickle cell drug
description: The study failure costs Agios a chance to one-up Novo Nordisk and represents yet another setback in a disease that’s frustrated drugmakers of late.

title: Novo sues Lilly, alleging ‘misleading’ advertising of obesity drugs
description: The lawsuit follows a cease-and-desist request and argues Lilly is using “deceptive” ads to claim its GLP-1 products are broadly superior to Novo’s medicines. Lilly countered that its campaign is “truthful.”

title: Dimension restocks with $800M to capitalize on AI’s ‘rapid ascent’
description: The young venture firm, which has bet heavily on tech and biotech’s convergence, has now invested in 35 companies and recently saw one of them acquired by AI giant Anthropic.

title: Novartis climbs on upbeat drug sales as key readouts loom
description: A return to quarterly sales growth boosted shares higher on Tuesday, though a handful of coming study results will have a bigger impact on the company’s long-term outlook.
